title: obd2.sim_scenario.v1
description: A synthetic ELM327 conversation used to emulate an OBD2 adapter and to act as a regression oracle. See SPEC.md.

scenario_id.description: Must match the file name without the .scenario.json suffix.

synthetic.description: Must be true. Emulated bytes must never masquerade as real vehicle data.

adapter_profile.description: Free-form label for the adapter behavior being emulated, e.g. "elm327_like_tcp".

at_response.description: Reply for any AT... command with no matching entry.

obd_response.description: Reply for any non-AT (OBD) request with no matching entry.

stream_split_bytes.description: If set, split every reply into chunks of at most this many bytes (simulates BLE notification / TCP segmentation sizes).

warnings.description: Informational notes about intentional quirks in this scenario.

request.description: Matched case-insensitively and ignoring all whitespace.

response_chunks.description: Reply payload pieces, sent in order. \r and \n are literal. Joined, then split by stream_split_bytes and any host split pattern.

delay_ms.description: Delay before the first chunk of this reply.

echo.description: Prepend the normalized request (plus \r) to the reply, like ELM echo.

prompt.description: Whether this reply ends at the ELM prompt '>'. Used by the validator to check chunk content.

post_action.description: none: normal. stall: send nothing (force a client timeout). disconnect: close the connection after any chunks.

repeat.description: When true, this entry keeps answering on every later match (pins the cursor). The last matching entry also repeats by default.

expectedScanSummary.description: Consumer-defined expectations. The emulator never interprets these; a scan/decoder asserts them so the scenario is self-checking.
